Overall:
The following processes are considered effective for the removal of diazinon: chlorine (up to 100 percent), hydrogen peroxide (up to 90 percent), ozone (65 to 100 percent), ozone  hydrogen peroxide (up to 70 percent), ultraviolet irradiation (up to 100 percent),ultraviolet irradiation hydrogen peroxide (upto 100 percent) and other treatments like irradiation with X-rays or &gamma-rays and ultrasound (upto 100 percent). Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
